title: CBIC Mandates Item-Level Declaration for Hazardous Cargo in Bills of Entry from July 1, 2026
content:
# CBIC Mandates Item-Level Declaration for Hazardous Cargo in Bills of Entry from July 1, 2026

Ek important update hai for all importers dealing with hazardous goods. Government ne system-based mechanism introduce kiya hai for identification and expedited customs clearance of such shipments.

The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has issued Circular No. 24/2026-Customs, dated May 14, 2026, making it mandatory to declare hazardous cargo at the item level in Bills of Entry starting from July 1, 2026. This move comes after several representations from the trade seeking a more efficient system for identifying hazardous consignments to avoid delays during customs clearance.

Here are the key points:

*   **Mandatory Declaration:** Importers will now have to mandatorily declare hazardous cargo at the item level in their Bills of Entry if the goods fall under the specified Customs Tariff Headings (CTH).
*   **System-Based Flagging:** Once declared, the customs system will automatically flag these Bills of Entry. This will alert customs officers for swift assessment, examination, and out-of-charge stages, ensuring faster processing.
*   **Consultation & List:** CBIC, in consultation with Mumbai Customs Zone II and industry representatives, has already compiled a list of hazardous chemicals and goods that require this mandatory declaration.
*   **RMS Amendments:** The National Customs Targeting Centre (NCTC) will also amend the Risk Management System (RMS) to further streamline the clearance process for identified hazardous goods.

This initiative aims to enhance trade facilitation by providing a clear, automated mechanism for handling hazardous imports.

## Read the Official Document
* [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.cbic.gov.in/)

*Published on: 16 May 2026*
